info.title: camofox-browser
info.description: Anti-detection browser automation server for AI agents. Accessibility snapshots, element refs, session isolation, cookie import, proxy rotation, and structured logs.

tags.name: Tabs
tags.description: Create, list, inspect, and destroy browser tabs.

tags.name: Navigation
tags.description: Navigate tabs to URLs or via search macros.

tags.name: Interaction
tags.description: Click, type, scroll, press keys, evaluate JS.

tags.name: Content
tags.description: Accessibility snapshots, screenshots, links, images, downloads.

BearerAuth.description: Bearer token matching CAMOFOX_API_KEY (per-route auth for sensitive endpoints like cookie import and traces).

AccessKeyAuth.description: Bearer token matching CAMOFOX_ACCESS_KEY. When set, gates all routes except /health, cookie import, and /stop. Acts as a superkey -- also accepted by endpoints that normally require CAMOFOX_API_KEY.

post.summary: Import cookies into a user session
post.description: Import cookies for authenticated browsing. Requires BearerAuth in production.

get.description: Detailed health with tab/session counts and failure tracking.
Returns Prometheus text exposition format. Requires PROMETHEUS_ENABLED=1.

post.description:
Closes tabs observed idle across multiple checks while preserving tabs
with active/queued operations. Never returns URLs, titles, cookies,
page text, or user IDs. Defaults to dry-run mode.

dryRun.description: When true, returns candidates without closing them.

minIdleMs.description: Minimum idle time (ms) before a tab is eligible.

maxTabsToClose.description: Maximum tabs to close per invocation.

minTabsPerSession.description: Preserve at least this many tabs per session.

closeEmptySessions.description: Close sessions left with zero tabs after cleanup.

200.description: Cleanup result with before/after counts and hashed metadata.

post.description: Creates a tab in the given session. Optionally navigates to an initial URL.

trace.description: Enable Playwright tracing for this session (screenshots, DOM snapshots, network). Must be set on first tab creation; cannot be added to an existing session.

409.description: Cannot enable tracing on an existing session.

get.description: Returns all tabs for a given userId.

post.summary: Navigate a tab to a URL or macro
post.description: Navigate to a URL or expand a search macro. Auto-creates tab if not found.

get.description: Returns accessibility tree with element refs. Supports pagination via offset.

post.summary: Wait for a selector or timeout

post.description: Click by element ref, CSS selector, or coordinates.

200.description: Click result with optional post-action snapshot.

post.summary: Attach a file to an upload control
post.description: Attaches a file to a file-upload control without going through the native OS file dialog. Two strategies are tried in order: (1) if an <input type="file"> is already present, call Playwright setInputFiles on it directly (works for hidden inputs); (2) otherwise arm a filechooser listener, activate the trigger element (ref or selector) via keyboard (focus + Enter) then a forced click as fallback, and call setFiles on the resulting chooser. The path(s) MUST be visible inside the container (e.g. a bind-mounted directory).

path.description: Absolute container-side path, or array of paths.

ref.description: Trigger element ref (e.g. e36). Optional when an input[type=file] already exists.

selector.description: Trigger element CSS/Playwright selector. Optional when an input[type=file] already exists.

400.description: Bad request (missing path/userId or file not found in container).

post.description: Types text into a focused element or a specific ref/selector.
Physically resizes the page via Playwright's `page.setViewportSize`, triggering a real layout reflow. Use for responsive testing — `window.resizeTo()` is a no-op on non-popup windows.

400.description: Width or height missing or out of range.

get.description: Returns tab metadata including URL, tool call count, visited URLs, download/failure counts.

post.description: Runs arbitrary JS in the page context and returns the result.

post.summary: Structured data extraction via JSON Schema
post.description:
Extracts structured data from the current page using a JSON Schema whose properties
carry `x-ref` hints pointing at snapshot element refs (e.g. `e1`, `e2`).  
Call `GET /tabs/{tabId}/snapshot` first to populate the ref table.

schema.description:
JSON Schema with `type: "object"` and a `properties` map.  
Each property may include `x-ref` (a snapshot element ref) and an optional
`type` (`string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean`).

required.description: Property names that must resolve to a non-null value.

data.description: Extracted key-value pairs matching the input schema.

400.description: Missing userId, missing schema, or invalid schema.

409.description: No refs available -- call snapshot first.

422.description: Extraction failed (e.g. required ref not found).

delete.summary: Close all tabs in a group

get.description: Returns all Playwright trace zip files for the given user session, sorted newest first.
Streams a Playwright trace zip for viewing in trace.playwright.dev.

delete.description: Removes a specific Playwright trace zip from the server.
Closes all tabs and cleans up state for the given userId.

get.description: Returns basic server liveness and browser state.

post.description: Ensures the browser process is running. Idempotent.
Stops the browser and closes all sessions. Requires x-admin-key header.
Navigate with targetId in body instead of path param.

get.description: Snapshot with targetId/userId as query params.

post.description: Routes to click/type/scroll/press/etc based on "kind" parameter.

kind.description: Action kind: click, type, scroll, press, key, select_option, drag, hover, screenshot, wait, back, forward.
